mechanism_narrative: Continuously rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending that drives corporate earnings and supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level vs. 20-day forward return is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 4.5% indicates a very tight labor market that forces wage inflation, pressures Fed to tighten, and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In months with UNRATE below 4.5%, the proportion of positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.5% concurrently with CPI annual inflation above 3%, real interest rates turn negative, creating a risk of overheating and eventual tightening that spooks equity markets over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In months meeting the trigger, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not negative with 95% confidence (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside sluggish payroll growth signals productivity gains without commensurate hiring, which depresses wage income and final demand, ultimately weighing on equity returns over the coming quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 2% and PAYEMS year-over-year growth < 1%
failure_condition: In quarters when the trigger is met, the median 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in retail sales above 1% provides a short-term positive impulse to equity prices as markets extrapolate strong consumer demand into immediate earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year window, the 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high housing starts reflect strong residential investment and household wealth creation that fuel economic momentum and support S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST levels and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (|r| < 0.05) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust payroll gains combined with a rising federal funds rate signal that the economy is overheating and the Fed is actively withdrawing accommodation, leading to tighter financial conditions and negative equity returns over the near term.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200,000 and FEDFUNDS > prior month value
failure_condition: In months meeting the trigger, the proportion of positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP is above its four-quarter moving average (indicating above-trend growth), a rising industrial production index confirms strong aggregate supply that boosts corporate profits and equity returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: In quarters where the trigger is active, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive with 95% confidence.
